claim_bullets.bullet_text: JavaScript uses the function keyword to name functions, and until ECMAScript 2015, this was the usual syntax for writing functions (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-65).
Functions written with the function keyword introduce a function with the function keyword, and they require a block and the return keyword to return values (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-65).
Functions written with the function keyword can be named, where the name is separate from the binding name in the environment (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-65).
The function keyword can be used to write functions that are not named, but it can also be used to name functions, which is useful for debugging and recursion (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-65).

source_record_text: JavaScript does have a syntax for naming a function, we use the function keyword. Until ECMAScript 2015 was created, function was the usual syntax for writing functions.
